boundary: Synthetic public examples only. Do not add private logs, connector exports, credentials, local paths, or personal data.

journeys.name: Start and direct
journeys.description: Describe the outcome, name trusted sources, set authority boundaries, and create a reviewable project shape.

journeys.name: Bound and prove
journeys.description: Keep proposed work inside evidence and authority boundaries, then leave inspectable proof.

journeys.name: Evaluate and operate
journeys.description: Test retrieval, generated systems, evaluation protocols, model workloads, and deterministic state.

maturity_definitions.flagship: The most complete public implementation in the toolkit, with a documented integration surface.
maturity_definitions.stable: A focused example with deterministic checks and a deliberately narrow public contract.
maturity_definitions.experimental: A tested teaching pattern whose contract may still change as outside use reveals better boundaries.

repos.name: Agent Operator Handbook
repos.use_when: Turn an idea or recurring job into bounded, reviewable work.
repos.proof: The starter bundle contains the declared source, authority, review, and handoff files and passes its structural checks.
repos.limitation: Guidance and templates do not enforce permissions or verify a live project.

repos.name: Reliable AI Work Starter
repos.use_when: Create a private file-based workspace with explicit sources, authority, review, and handoff state.
repos.proof: The declared starter files, setup boundary, template links, and public-safety text pass deterministic structural checks.
repos.limitation: The starter does not run an agent, enforce permissions, inspect sources, or prove that a live workflow is useful.
repos.operation: Edits three named local files after approval

repos.name: Codex Project Instructions Starter
repos.use_when: Give a coding agent clear project rules before it works.
repos.proof: The required instruction templates and examples are present and structurally valid.
repos.limitation: Written instructions guide behavior but do not technically enforce it.

repos.name: Public Repo Safety Kit
repos.use_when: Check a public-candidate repo before publishing.
repos.proof: The checked tree avoids the kit's known private-path, credential, identity, and publication-risk cases.
repos.limitation: A pass is not a complete security review and does not grant permission to publish.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: EvidenceGate
repos.use_when: Leave a compact review receipt for AI-assisted work.
repos.proof: Declared claims, checks, changed paths, review, and public-safety fields are tied to the exact Git revision supplied to the verifier.
repos.limitation: It does not authenticate a reviewer, prove semantic correctness, or approve publication.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Local Model Reliability Example
repos.use_when: Validate structured local-model output before trusting it.
repos.proof: Synthetic outputs satisfy the declared shape, citation, confidence, and no-write contracts.
repos.limitation: The example calls no model and does not measure live-model quality.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Context Boundary Examples
repos.use_when: Check that answers stay inside supplied evidence.
repos.proof: Expected answers cite only allowed sources and known unsupported or uncited outputs fail.
repos.limitation: Grounding to supplied snippets does not establish that those snippets are true or current.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Agent Action Authority Examples
repos.use_when: Classify model or agent actions before execution.
repos.proof: Synthetic actions and scoped grants receive the expected allow, reject, or reapproval decisions.
repos.limitation: The classifier does not execute actions, provide a sandbox, or infer security-relevant scope.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Green-Spine QA Pattern
repos.use_when: Keep one important workflow obviously healthy.
repos.proof: One representative synthetic path and its known-bad cases satisfy the named checkpoint.
repos.limitation: A green spine deliberately does not prove every feature, path, or experience quality.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: SQLite Context Retrieval Example
repos.use_when: Turn wrong-scope, stale-source, and authority retrieval failures into regression buckets.
repos.proof: The synthetic failure registry selects or refuses correctly and keeps critical buckets independently green.
repos.limitation: The fixtures are synthetic and natural-language-to-filter translation is outside the evaluated boundary.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Sealed Evaluation Pattern
repos.use_when: Check access order, frozen outputs, digests, and retirement of revealed calibration material.
repos.proof: The supplied record preserves the declared information zones, freeze order, digests, and retirement rule.
repos.limitation: The checker is not a sandbox and cannot prove an access log is complete or authentic.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Generated-System QA Pattern
repos.use_when: Check generated-data freshness, integrity, reachability, required services, and a representative journey.
repos.proof: The supplied artifact matches its blueprint and satisfies the declared structural and journey requirements.
repos.limitation: Structural readiness does not prove live UI freshness, domain quality, accessibility, or enjoyment.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: Model Workload Telemetry
repos.use_when: Report completion, cost, time, quality, and revision burden within paired workload classes.
repos.proof: Only task instances attempted by every compared model contribute to each workload report.
repos.limitation: The report does not prove causal model superiority, cost efficiency, or statistical significance.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

repos.name: AI Game State Machine Pattern
repos.use_when: Keep one authoritative set of legal commands and unresolved obligations across save and replay.
repos.proof: Illegal actions remain non-mutating, inspection is read-only, obligations persist, and seeded replay is deterministic.
repos.limitation: State-machine correctness does not prove that the game is understandable, balanced, emotional, or fun.
repos.operation: Read-only check; examples may use temporary files

action.label: Open AI Game State Machine Pattern
